teachings.teaching: Self-sacrificial death is presented as a purposeful act that leads to honor and reward (division of spoil/portion with the great).
Being “numbered with transgressors” implies solidarity with the wrongdoing—resting within a context of sin rather than avoiding it.
One person’s bearing of “the sin of many” teaches that guilt can be carried by another on behalf of many.
Intercession for transgressors is emphasized as a continuing consequence of that bearing—moving beyond judgment toward advocacy/appeal.
The text frames evil (sin and transgression) as something that can be addressed through a sacrificial, redemptive act rather than only through punishment.

goals.goal: To explain the redemptive meaning of a sacrificial death—how it results in vindication and divine favor, involves taking sin on behalf of many, and leads to intercession for transgressors.
